content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     Personal     Youth banking     Banking for teens     Debit cards for teens   Choosing a debit card as a teen   Tips and tricks to help you manage your money with a debit card.    What is a debit card?   A debit card is a card that’s linked to your everyday transaction account. It lets you easily access your own money from the account at an ATM or when shopping. NAB offers two debit cards that are suitable for teenagers.    Debit cards for kids under 13    A NAB Debit Card is available to customers aged 8 to 13 years. This card allows you to withdraw money from your account at an ATM.    *  View Disclaimer 1 You can make purchases at shops using EFTPOS, but you can’t shop online or add the card to your digital wallet.     You can apply for a NAB Debit Card by visiting a branch.     NAB Visa Debit card    A NAB Visa Debit card is available to customers aged 14 years and older. With this card you can:     withdraw from or deposit into your account at an ATM    *  View Disclaimer 1  make purchases at shops  shop online  add the card to your digital wallet.    Debit card tips for teens   A debit card is a great way to learn how to manage your own money. You can use your debit card to access the money in your transaction account, such as your salary from an after-school job.    Here are our tips for using your first debit card smartly and safely.     Activate your card. Sign the back of your physical debit card. Use our help guide to activate your new card and set a PIN. Keep your PIN secret – don’t share it with anyone or write it down.  Keep your card details safe. Keep your card details secret – don’t share them with friends or show them on social media. Someone else could use this information to shop online, even if they don’t have the physical card. Learn about staying safe on social media and keeping your devices and apps safe.  Start banking online. Register for internet banking and download the NAB app to easily manage your card. Learn how to create strong passwords and keep them safe.  Add your card to a digital wallet. Learn how to add your debit card to Apple Pay, Google Pay and other digital wallets so you can pay with your phone. You need to be at least 13 to add a card to Apple Pay and 16 to add a card to Google Pay.  Track your spending. The Spending feature in the NAB app makes it easier to understand where your money is going.  Shop safely online. Look for a small lock symbol next to the URL which indicates a website is safe. Learn more about staying safe when you shop online.  Avoid overspending. Remember, you can only spend the money in your linked transaction account. If you try to spend more than your available balance the transaction might be declined. You can set up notifications in the NAB app or NAB Internet Banking to let you know when your account is overdrawn or your balance is getting low.     What to do if something goes wrong   Regularly checking your spending on the NAB app is a great way to keep track of your money and can help you notice early if something isn’t right.    If you see a suspicious transaction in your account, or if your card is lost or stolen:     Block your card immediately in the NAB app or Internet Banking. Learn how to block your card on your iPhone or Android phoneTM.   Call us on 13 22 65.    Products you might be interested in   Learn about our bank accounts and debit cards for teens.    NAB Visa Debit cards   Access your own money wherever you are, however you bank.    NAB Classic Banking account   However you bank, you’ll pay no monthly account fee, ever. Spend more on what’s important to you.    Banking for teens   Helping teenagers with their banking needs.    Money tips for teens   Advice to help teens and students manage their money.    International student banking   Here’s what international students need to know about banking in Australia.    How to lodge your first tax return   Here’s the information you’ll need to supply when you file your first tax return.    What is a savings account?   Learn how a savings account works, and why it might be the best option to help you reach your money goals.    Important Information   Terms and Conditions         Disclaimer 1   ATM fees may apply when using your card at ATMs operated by other ATM networks, including overseas ATMs. When using your card for overseas ATM withdrawals, an International Transaction Fee and an ATM cash withdrawal fee applies. Overseas ATM operators may charge you a separate fee to use their ATMs.     This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.
